2606.30417.title: Beyond Point Estimates for Glaucoma Visual Field Forecasting with Diffusion Models
2606.30417.paper_summary: Forecasting visual fields (VFs) is critical for personalized monitoring and treatment planning in glaucoma. This is inherently uncertain due to heterogeneous disease progression and measurement variability, yet most existing methods produce single deterministic predictions that fail to represent this uncertainty. We formulate VF forecasting as a probabilistic prediction problem and the use of conditioned denoising diffusion models to generate distributions of plausible future VFs from longitudinal observations with irregular follow-up intervals. Experiments on two independent VF cohorts show that diffusion-based predictions produce well-calibrated distributions for clinically relevant VF measures. When reduced to a standard point-estimate, the proposed approach achieves state-of-the-art accuracy compared to clinical baselines and prior learning-based methods. Our results highlight the advantages of distributional modeling for VF forecasting and support a shift from point-estimate prediction toward uncertainty-aware, clinically interpretable risk assessment in glaucoma.
2606.30417.paper_summary_zh: 預測視野 (VFs) 對於青光眼的個性化監測和治療計劃至關重要。由於疾病進展的異質性和測量變異性，這本質上是充滿不確定性的，然而大多數現有方法產生的都是單一的確定性預測，無法表現出這種不確定性。我們將視野預測形式化為一個概率預測問題，並使用條件去噪擴散模型從具有不規則隨訪間隔的縱向觀察中生成合理未來視野的分佈。對兩個獨立視野隊列的實驗顯示，基於擴散的預測為臨床相關的視野指標產生了良好校準的分佈。當簡化為標準點估計時，所提出的方法與臨床基準和先前基於學習的方法相比，達到了最先進的準確性。我們的結果突顯了分佈建模在視野預測中的優勢，並支持從點估計預測轉向對不確定性感知的、臨床可解釋的青光眼風險評估。
2606.30417.author: Marta Colmenar Herrera et.al.
2606.30417.authors: Marta Colmenar Herrera, Pablo Márquez Neila, Şerife Seda Kucur Ergünay, Martin S. Zinkernagel, Raphael Sznitman
